repository.name: MysticalWorld
repository.description: A top-down RPG inspired by The Legend of Zelda: A Link to the Past. Built with Phaser.js and vanilla JavaScript.

milestones.title: M1 – Playable Foundation
milestones.description: Project runs in browser. Hero walks on a tile map. Tests infrastructure in place.

milestones.title: M2 – Core RPG Systems
milestones.description: Combat, HP, currency, items, equipment and vendor all functional with unit tests.

milestones.title: M3 – Full UI
milestones.description: HUD, inventory screen, dialogue box and shop UI all complete.

milestones.title: M4 – World & Dungeons
milestones.description: Full overworld, one village, one dungeon with boss. Save/load working.

milestones.title: M5 – Polish & E2E
milestones.description: E2E tests passing, world content complete, game shippable.

issues.title: Phase 1 – Project foundation and folder structure
issues.body:
## Goal
Set up the complete project skeleton so all future phases have a home.

## Tasks
- [ ] Create folder structure: `characters/`, `characters/enemies/`, `characters/npcs/`, `maps/`, `maps/dungeons/`, `ui/`, `persistence/`, `assets/sprites/`, `assets/tilesets/`, `assets/sounds/`
- [ ] Create `index.html` — loads Phaser.js via CDN, mounts the canvas, links `css/ui.css` and `js/main.js`
- [ ] Create `css/ui.css` — base styles for HTML overlays (loading screen, menu wrappers)
- [ ] Create `js/main.js` — Phaser game config (width: 800, height: 600, pixelArt: true, scene list)
- [ ] Create placeholder `README.md` with project overview and how to run
- [ ] Create `CLAUDE.md` with architecture, folder roles, and logic-vs-Phaser separation rule

## Acceptance Criteria
- Opening `index.html` in a browser shows a black Phaser canvas with no console errors
- All folders exist with a `.gitkeep` so they are tracked by git

## Notes
Phaser.js version: 3.x loaded from CDN. No build step required for the game itself — keep it runnable by opening `index.html` directly.

issues.title: Phase 2 – Test infrastructure (Vitest + Playwright)
issues.body:
## Goal
Set up testing before any game logic is written so every system is testable from day one.

## Tasks
- [ ] Create `package.json` with dev dependencies: `vitest`, `@playwright/test`, `jsdom`
- [ ] Create `vitest.config.js` — use `jsdom` environment, include `tests/unit/**` and `tests/integration/**`
- [ ] Create `playwright.config.js` — base URL `http://localhost:8080`, test dir `tests/e2e/`
- [ ] Create folder structure: `tests/unit/`, `tests/integration/`, `tests/e2e/`
- [ ] Add npm scripts: `test` (vitest run), `test:watch` (vitest), `test:e2e` (playwright), `test:all` (both)
- [ ] Create a simple sanity test `tests/unit/sanity.test.js` that asserts `1 + 1 === 2`
- [ ] Confirm `npm test` passes

## Acceptance Criteria
- `npm test` runs Vitest and passes
- `npm run test:e2e` runs Playwright (may fail until game is running — that is OK)
- No Phaser or browser canvas is needed to run unit tests

## Architecture Rule
All game logic must be implemented as **pure JS classes** (no `new Phaser.*` inside them). Phaser scenes are thin view wrappers only. This separation is what makes unit tests possible.

issues.title: Phase 3 – Tilemap rendering system
issues.body:
## Goal
Render a scrollable tile-based overworld map with collision.

## Tasks
- [ ] Create `maps/MapManager.js` (pure JS) — loads map JSON, exposes tile data, handles scene transitions
- [ ] Create `maps/GameScene.js` (Phaser.Scene) — renders tilemap, sets up camera, calls MapManager
- [ ] Create a sample `maps/overworld.json` — minimal Tiled-compatible map (32×32 tiles, 40×30 grid)
- [ ] Add a collision tile layer — tiles marked as blocked stop the player
- [ ] Camera follows the hero with world bounds set to map size
- [ ] Create `assets/tilesets/overworld.png` placeholder (colored tiles: green=grass, blue=water, brown=dirt)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Overworld map renders correctly in the browser
- Camera scrolls as the player moves
- Collision tiles block movement

## Tile Size
32×32 pixels (easier to work with at modern screen sizes while keeping the retro look).

issues.title: Phase 4 – Hero character (movement + animations)
issues.body:
## Goal
A controllable hero character that walks around the map with smooth animations.

## Tasks
- [ ] Create `characters/HeroState.js` (pure JS) — manages position, direction, speed, HP, stats, inventory reference. No Phaser.
- [ ] Create `characters/Hero.js` (Phaser.GameObjects.Sprite) — reads input, calls HeroState, plays animations
- [ ] 4-directional movement (up/down/left/right) via arrow keys and WASD
- [ ] Idle animation (1 frame per direction)
- [ ] Walk animation (4 frames per direction = 16 total frames)
- [ ] Sword swing animation (4 frames per direction)
- [ ] Hero stops at collision tiles
- [ ] Create placeholder spritesheet `assets/sprites/hero.png` (colored rectangles per frame)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Hero walks in 4 directions with correct animation
- Hero cannot walk through collision tiles
- Switching direction immediately plays correct animation

## Architecture Note
`HeroState.js` must be importable in Vitest without any Phaser context.

issues.title: Phase 5 – RPG health system
issues.body:
## Goal
A proper RPG HP system — not heart containers. HP is numeric, scalable, and displayed as a bar.

## Tasks
- [ ] Add to `HeroState.js`: `hp`, `maxHp`, `takeDamage(amount)`, `heal(amount)`, `isDead()`, `increaseMaxHp(amount)`
- [ ] HP is clamped: `0 <= hp <= maxHp`
- [ ] Create `characters/StatusEffects.js` (pure JS) — poison, regeneration tick logic
- [ ] Potions: small potion restores 20 HP, large potion restores full HP
- [ ] Death state: hero stops, death animation plays, respawn at last save point
- [ ] Create `tests/unit/HeroState.test.js` covering: takeDamage, heal, death threshold, maxHp scaling

## Acceptance Criteria
- `npm test` passes all HeroState HP tests
- HP never goes below 0 or above maxHp
- Hero death triggers correctly at hp === 0

issues.title: Phase 6 – Combat system
issues.body:
## Goal
Sword-based melee combat with damage formula, hitboxes, and invincibility frames.

## Tasks
- [ ] Create `js/CombatCalculator.js` (pure JS) — `calcDamage(attacker, defender)`: `max(1, attack - defense)`, crit (1.5×, 10% chance)
- [ ] Hero sword swing: press Space or Z → swing animation → rectangular hitbox active for 200ms in facing direction
- [ ] On hitbox overlap with enemy: call `CombatCalculator.calcDamage`, apply to enemy HP
- [ ] Enemy attacks hero: on contact, deal damage, hero gets 800ms invincibility frames (flashing sprite)
- [ ] Knockback: hit target moves back 32px in hit direction over 200ms
- [ ] Create `tests/unit/CombatCalculator.test.js`: damage formula, min 1 damage, crit range, invincibility timing

## Acceptance Criteria
- `npm test` passes all CombatCalculator tests
- Sword swing has correct hitbox and duration
- Invincibility frames prevent damage spam

issues.title: Phase 7 – Currency system (Silver & Gold)
issues.body:
## Goal
Silver and Gold coin currency. 100 Silver = 1 Gold. Auto-converts on pickup.

## Tasks
- [ ] Create `js/CurrencySystem.js` (pure JS): stores total value in silver internally, exposes `addSilver(n)`, `addGold(n)`, `spend(silver)`, `toDisplay()` → `{ gold, silver }`, `toString()` → `'3G 47S'`
- [ ] Auto-convert: when silver >= 100, convert to gold automatically
- [ ] `spend(amount)` returns `false` if insufficient funds (never go negative)
- [ ] Coins drop from enemies and chests as `{ silver: n }` or `{ gold: n }` loot entries
- [ ] Coin pickup animation: floating text shows `+5S` or `+1G`
- [ ] Create `tests/unit/CurrencySystem.test.js`: conversion, display, spend, edge cases (exact 100S, 0 balance, insufficient funds)

## Acceptance Criteria
- `npm test` passes all CurrencySystem tests
- 100S auto-converts to 1G correctly
- Player cannot spend more than they have

issues.title: Phase 8 – Enemy system
issues.body:
## Goal
Enemy base class with AI state machine, plus two concrete enemies.

## Tasks
- [ ] Create `characters/enemies/EnemyState.js` (pure JS) — AI states: `idle`, `patrol`, `chase`, `attack`, `flee`, `dead`. State transition logic.
- [ ] Create `characters/enemies/Enemy.js` (Phaser.Sprite) — renders enemy, runs EnemyState tick
- [ ] AI behaviours: patrol (random walk), chase (move toward hero when within 150px), attack (melee on contact)
- [ ] Drop table: each enemy has a `drops` array of `{ type, chance, value }` entries
- [ ] Create `characters/enemies/Slime.js` — slow, low HP, drops silver
- [ ] Create `characters/enemies/Darknut.js` — armored, high defense, drops gold
- [ ] Create `tests/unit/EnemyState.test.js`: state transitions, drop roll logic

## Acceptance Criteria
- Enemies patrol when hero is far, chase when close, attack on contact
- Drops spawn correctly on death
- `npm test` passes all EnemyState tests

issues.title: Phase 9 – Item and equipment system
issues.body:
## Goal
Items with stats. Equipment slots. Equip/unequip recalculates hero stats.

## Tasks
- [ ] Create `js/ItemRegistry.js` (pure JS) — master list of all items: `{ id, name, type, slot, stats, description, buyPrice, sellPrice, icon }`
- [ ] Item types: `weapon`, `shield`, `armor`, `accessory`, `consumable`
- [ ] Equipment slots: `weapon`, `shield`, `armor`, `accessory` (one item per slot)
- [ ] Create `js/InventoryManager.js` (pure JS): `addItem(id)`, `removeItem(id)`, `equipItem(id)`, `unequipItem(slot)`, `getEquipped()`, inventory max size (24 slots)
- [ ] `StatSystem.js`: recalculates `attack`, `defense`, `speed`, `maxHp` from base stats + equipped item bonuses
- [ ] Create `tests/unit/InventoryManager.test.js`: add/remove, equip/unequip, full inventory limit, wrong slot type rejection, stat recalculation

## Acceptance Criteria
- `npm test` passes all InventoryManager and StatSystem tests
- Equipping a weapon increases attack stat immediately
- Cannot equip armor in weapon slot

issues.title: Phase 10 – Inventory UI screen
issues.body:
## Goal
Pause menu showing inventory grid, equipment slots, item tooltips and actions.

## Tasks
- [ ] Create `ui/Inventory.js` (Phaser.Scene, runs over game scene) — opens on Escape/I key
- [ ] Left panel: 24-slot grid showing carried items with icon + quantity
- [ ] Right panel: equipment slots diagram (head, weapon, shield, armor, accessory)
- [ ] Hover/select item: show tooltip (name, stats, description, buy/sell price)
- [ ] Item actions on click: Equip, Use (consumable), Drop, Sell (only near vendor)
- [ ] Keyboard navigation supported (arrow keys + Enter)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Inventory opens and closes without breaking game state
- Equipping an item from inventory moves it to the equipment slot
- Tooltip shows correct stats for all item types

issues.title: Phase 11 – Vendor / shop system
issues.body:
## Goal
Vendor NPCs that buy and sell items. Player can sell items for silver/gold.

## Tasks
- [ ] Create `js/VendorSystem.js` (pure JS): `buyItem(itemId, inventory, currency)`, `sellItem(itemId, inventory, currency)`. Sell price = 50% of buy price (floored). Returns `{ success, reason }`.
- [ ] Create `ui/ShopUI.js` (Phaser.Scene overlay) — two-column layout: vendor stock (left), player inventory (right), currency display
- [ ] Vendor stock is defined per NPC instance in map data
- [ ] Insufficient funds shows error message, item is not transferred
- [ ] Create `tests/unit/VendorSystem.test.js`: buy success, buy insufficient funds, sell success, sell price formula

## Acceptance Criteria
- `npm test` passes all VendorSystem tests
- Player can buy and sell items with correct currency changes
- Shop UI opens when interacting with a vendor NPC

issues.title: Phase 12 – HUD (heads-up display)
issues.body:
## Goal
Always-visible HUD: HP bar, currency, equipped items.

## Tasks
- [ ] Create `ui/HUD.js` (Phaser.Scene, fixed camera, runs in parallel with game scene)
- [ ] HP bar: colored bar (green→yellow→red based on % HP) with `HP: 45/80` text
- [ ] Currency display: coin icon + `3G 47S`
- [ ] Two equipped item slots (weapon + secondary): icon + name
- [ ] HP bar animates smoothly when HP changes
- [ ] HUD updates reactively when `HeroState` changes (event emitter)

## Acceptance Criteria
- HUD is always visible during gameplay (not hidden on scene change)
- HP bar correctly reflects current HP and changes in real time
- Currency updates immediately after pickup or purchase

issues.title: Phase 13 – NPC and dialogue system
issues.body:
## Goal
Talkable NPCs with a text dialogue box and action branching.

## Tasks
- [ ] Create `characters/npcs/NPC.js` (Phaser.Sprite) — idle animation, proximity indicator (press E icon)
- [ ] Create `ui/DialogBox.js` (Phaser.Scene overlay) — bottom-panel dialogue box, NPC name header, text paging (press E to advance), blinking cursor
- [ ] Dialogue data lives in `characters/npcs/dialogues/` as JSON: `[ { text, options: [{label, action}] } ]`
- [ ] Branching: options list renders as a choice menu (e.g. `[Talk] [Trade] [Goodbye]`)
- [ ] `Trade` option opens `ShopUI` for vendor NPCs
- [ ] Game movement pauses while dialogue is open

## Acceptance Criteria
- Player can walk up to NPC, press E, read dialogue, advance pages, and close
- Vendor branch opens shop correctly
- Movement is locked during dialogue

issues.title: Phase 14 – Dungeon system
issues.body:
## Goal
Room-based dungeon with locked doors, chests, keys and a boss room.

## Tasks
- [ ] Create `maps/dungeons/DungeonManager.js` (pure JS) — room state (visited, cleared, locked), key count, mini-map data
- [ ] Room transition: screen fade when hero reaches a door tile, load next room scene, position hero at entry point
- [ ] Locked doors require 1 key item; door opens permanently for that save
- [ ] Chests: one-time loot (item or currency), state saved so re-entering room doesn't re-spawn chest
- [ ] Mini-map overlay: shows rooms as rectangles, current room highlighted, visited rooms visible
- [ ] Boss room: special room with boss enemy, locked until all prior rooms are cleared
- [ ] Create `maps/dungeons/dungeon1.json` — 6-room dungeon layout

## Acceptance Criteria
- Rooms transition correctly and hero spawns at the correct door
- Locked doors open with a key
- Chest state persists across room re-entries within a session

issues.title: Phase 15 – Save and load system
issues.body:
## Goal
Persistent save slots using localStorage. Auto-save on room transition.

## Tasks
- [ ] Create `persistence/SaveManager.js` (pure JS): `save(slot, gameState)`, `load(slot)` → gameState or null, `deleteSave(slot)`, `listSlots()`. Uses `JSON.stringify/parse`. 3 slots.
- [ ] `gameState` snapshot includes: hero HP/maxHp/position/stats, inventory, equipped items, currency, map ID, room ID, visited rooms, chest states, quest flags
- [ ] Auto-save triggers on every room/scene transition
- [ ] Corrupt/missing save: `load()` returns `null`, game shows 'New Game' option gracefully
- [ ] Create `ui/SaveSlotUI.js` — save slot selection screen shown on game boot
- [ ] Create `tests/unit/SaveManager.test.js`: save/load roundtrip, all 3 slots, corrupt JSON fallback, slot overwrite
- [ ] Create `tests/integration/SaveLoad.test.js`: full game state → save → mutate state → load → state matches original

## Acceptance Criteria
- `npm test` passes all SaveManager and SaveLoad integration tests
- Reloading the page and selecting a save slot restores exact game state

issues.title: Phase 16 – E2E tests (Playwright)
issues.body:
## Goal
End-to-end browser tests confirming the game boots and core flows work.

## Tasks
- [ ] Set up a local static file server for Playwright to use (e.g. `npx serve .` or `npx http-server`)
- [ ] `tests/e2e/boot.test.js`: game loads, canvas is visible, no console errors
- [ ] `tests/e2e/hero-movement.test.js`: pressing arrow key causes hero position to change
- [ ] `tests/e2e/hud.test.js`: HUD is visible after boot, HP bar renders
- [ ] `tests/e2e/save-load.test.js`: save game → reload page → load save → hero is at saved position
- [ ] `tests/e2e/vendor.test.js`: interact with vendor NPC → shop opens → buy item → currency decreases

## Acceptance Criteria
- `npm run test:e2e` passes all Playwright tests against locally served game
- No test relies on hardcoded pixel positions — use game state / DOM elements

issues.title: Phase 17 – World building (overworld + village + dungeon 1)
issues.body:
## Goal
A complete playable world: overworld, one village, one dungeon with a boss.

## Tasks
- [ ] Design full overworld map (80×60 tiles): open fields, forests, paths, water, mountains impassable
- [ ] Village area: 4–6 buildings, 3 NPCs (blacksmith vendor, healer, story NPC), inn (restores full HP for 10S)
- [ ] Place enemy spawn zones across overworld (Slimes in fields, Darknuts near dungeon entrance)
- [ ] Connect overworld to dungeon entrance via door tile on map
- [ ] Dungeon 1: 6 rooms, locked doors, chests with sword upgrade and gold, boss enemy (large Darknut with 200 HP)
- [ ] Boss drops unique item on death
- [ ] Place save crystal in village (interact to save manually)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Player can walk from spawn → village → dungeon entrance → complete dungeon → defeat boss
- All NPCs have dialogue
- All chest/drop/vendor interactions work end-to-end

issues.title: Update CLAUDE.md with final architecture and commands
issues.body:
## Goal
Keep CLAUDE.md accurate and useful as the project grows.

## Tasks
- [ ] Document all npm scripts: `npm test`, `npm run test:watch`, `npm run test:e2e`, `npm run test:all`, `npm start` (static server)
- [ ] Document folder roles (one line each)
- [ ] Document the logic-vs-Phaser separation rule with an example
- [ ] Document item slot types and currency system rules
- [ ] Document save state shape

## Note
This issue should be updated (not closed and re-opened) as the project evolves. Keep CLAUDE.md reflecting current reality.

instructions_for_agent.summary: Use the GitHub CLI (gh) to create this project. Run each step in order.
steps: 1. Create the repository: gh repo create MysticalWorld --private --description '...' (use repository.description)
2. Clone it locally and push the existing files (index.html, CLAUDE.md, README.md, github-setup.json)
3. Create all labels: iterate labels array, run: gh label create '<name>' --color '<color>' --description '<description>'
4. Create all milestones: iterate milestones array, run: gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/milestones --method POST --field title='...' --field description='...'
5. Create all issues: for each issue in issues array, resolve milestone title to ID from step 4, then run: gh issue create --title '...' --body '...' --label '...' --milestone <id>
6. Issues should be created in order (they will be numbered #1 through #18 matching the phases)
notes: All label names in issues[] match exactly the names defined in labels[]
Milestone titles in issues[] match exactly the titles defined in milestones[]
The body fields use GitHub Flavored Markdown with task checkboxes
